task_completion.description: Create a new tiddler with the required title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a TiddlyWiki edit screen where the title field is set to '$:/custom/ProjectDashboard'.

task_completion.description: Add the required tag to make the tiddler appear in the sidebar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the tag '$:/tags/SideBar' added in the tags input area below the title field.

task_completion.description: Add a custom caption field to set the sidebar tab's display name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Add a new field' section at the bottom of the edit screen, showing a field named 'caption' with the value 'Projects'.

task_completion.description: Write the list widget in the tiddler body to dynamically list and sort the tiddlers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for TiddlyWiki list widget syntax in the text editor, which should include the filters 'tag[ProjectAlpha]' and 'sort[title]' (e.g., <$list filter="[tag[ProjectAlpha]sort[title]]">).

task_completion.description: Verify that the new 'Projects' tab appears in the sidebar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the right sidebar of the TiddlyWiki interface. A tab named 'Projects' should be visible alongside the default tabs like 'Open', 'Recent', 'Tools', etc.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Projects' tab displays the correct clickable links.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the content under the 'Projects' tab in the sidebar. It should display a list of tiddler titles formatted as clickable links.

integrity.description: The agent used the TiddlyWiki web interface to create the tiddler.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the TiddlyWiki web application in a browser, not terminal commands or a code editor modifying backend files.

integrity.description: The agent actually filled out the tiddler fields and saved it.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the progression of typing the title, tags, fields, and body content, followed by saving the tiddler.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the sidebar tab.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) should not be open and used to manually inject the 'Projects' tab into the DOM.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target tiddler must be titled '$:/custom/ProjectDashboard'. It must have the tag '$:/tags/SideBar' and a custom field 'caption' set to 'Projects'. The body must contain a list widget with a filter including 'tag[ProjectAlpha]' and 'sort[title]'. The final result should be a 'Projects' tab in the TiddlyWiki sidebar displaying the relevant links.
